Title: UGT73C6 and UGT78D1, Glycosyltransferases Involved in Flavonol Glycoside Biosynthesis in Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: UGT78D1 is a specific leverage point to reduce flavonol rhamnosylation and shift flux toward non-rhamnosylated or glucosylated flavonol glycosides, which the authors suggest could improve flavonol bioavailability. It is suitable for knockout/editing or allele selection, potentially combined with editing of the unidentified flavonol 7-O-rhamnosyltransferase for stronger reduction of rhamnose-containing flavonols. Deployment should consider changes in the overall glycoside profile rather than total flavonol only.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose to the 3-OH position of flavonols, notably quercetin and kaempferol, forming flavonol 3-O-rhamnosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of UGT78D1 abolishes flavonol-3-O-rhamnoside-7-O-rhamnosides in leaves and removes quercetin-3-O-rhamnoside-derived glycosides in flowers, demonstrating that UGT78D1 is required for 3-O-rhamnosylation steps leading to major Arabidopsis flavonol glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutant analysis; RT-PCR confirmation of transcript loss; LC-MS metabolite profiling of leaves and flowers; recombinant protein expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with UDP-rhamnose donor and flavonol substrates; comparison with authentic standards.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an immediate precursor for major flavonol diglycosides and directly contributes to terminal flavonol glycoside accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants were grown under unstressed growth-room conditions without sunlight/UV, 18 h light at 25°C; flavonoid profiles were analyzed in leaves and flowers under these conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed at similar levels in rosette leaves, flowers/inflorescences, siliques, and stems; only very low expression in roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in Arabidopsis leaves, flowers, siliques, and stems; low in roots.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: EST evidence for expression; LC-MS metabolite profiling. No public accession reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This study describes the first in vitro characterization of a plant natural product rhamnosyltransferase acting on flavonols. Arabidopsis does not appear to accumulate flavonol monoglycosides detectably in planta, but enzymology and mutant phenotypes together established function. Residual UDP-glucose in the UDP-rhamnose preparation allowed minor glucoside side products in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavonol 3-O-rhamnosylation branch of kaempferol and quercetin glycoside formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alters glycoside composition; other two main kaempferol glycosides increased in the mutant, indicating metabolic redistribution. No growth penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-defining glycosylation step for 3-O-rhamnosylated flavonols; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T-DNA disruption of UGT78D1 eliminated kaempferol-3-O-rhamnoside-7-O-rhamnoside from leaves and removed specific quercetin rhamnoside-containing glycosides from flowers. 2. Recombinant UGT78D1 transferred rhamnose from UDP-rhamnose to the 3-OH of quercetin and kaempferol in vitro. 3. UGT78D1 therefore encodes the Arabidopsis flavonol 3-O-rhamnosyltransferase that supplies precursors for major terminal flavonol glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonol glycosides, especially kaempferol and quercetin rhamnosylated glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant natural products implicated in plant metabolism and, in dietary context, proposed preventive effects against coronary disease; glycosylation influences flavonol bioavailability and biological effects.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: UGT73C6 can be targeted to modulate 7-O-glucosylation of flavonol 3-O-rhamnosides and thereby alter terminal flavonol glycoside composition. Engineering should preferably combine UGT73C6 with other glycosyltransferases in the pathway because the final profile depends on precursor availability and the whole GT complement. This gene is useful for tailoring glycoside structure rather than bulk flavonol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the 7-OH position of flavonol 3-O-rhamnosides, especially quercetin-3-O-rhamnoside and kaempferol-3-O-rhamnoside, producing flavonol-3-O-rhamnoside-7-O-glucosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of UGT73C6 abolishes quercetin-3-O-rhamnoside-7-O-glucoside accumulation in flowers, showing that UGT73C6 is required for 7-O-glucosylation of 3-O-rhamnosylated flavonols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutant analysis; RT-PCR confirmation of transcript loss; LC-MS metabolite profiling; recombinant protein expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with UDP-glucose donor and flavonol glycoside substrates; comparison with independently synthesized standard.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a terminal flavonol glycoside and modifies immediate flavonol glycoside precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants were grown under unstressed growth-room conditions without sunlight/UV, 18 h light at 25°C; leaves and flowers were analyzed under these conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression is largely restricted to leaves and flowers; only very low expression in roots and not prominent in siliques or stems.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed mainly in Arabidopsis leaves and flowers; low in roots.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: EST clone AV521229; LC-MS metabolite profiling. No public omics accession reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UGT73C6 showed broader in vitro acceptor flexibility than implied by the in planta phenotype, including activity toward several flavonoids and hydroxyflavones, but in planta its effect was precise. Activity toward glycosides was much higher than toward aglycones, supporting sequential 3-O-glycosylation before 7-O-glucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavonol 7-O-glucosylation branch acting on 3-O-rhamnosylated kaempferol/quercetin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes terminal flavonol glycoside pattern; no growth or fitness penalties reported. In vitro promiscuity suggests potential off-target glycosylation under heterologous engineering contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-specific downstream tailoring step; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ugt73C6 mutants lacked the quercetin-rhamnoside-glucoside peak found in wild-type flowers. 2. Recombinant UGT73C6 used UDP-glucose to glucosylate quercetin-3-O-rhamnoside and kaempferol-3-O-rhamnoside, with strong preference for glycosylated flavonol acceptors over aglycones. 3. UGT73C6 was identified as the Arabidopsis flavonol-3-O-glycoside 7-O-glucosyltransferase shaping terminal flavonol glycoside composition.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonol glycosides, especially quercetin-3-O-rhamnoside-7-O-glucoside and related kaempferol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant natural products whose glycosylation state influences physicochemical properties and, in dietary context, bioavailability and biological effects.
